channel.title: probability on DELs personal site
channel.description: Recent content in probability on DELs personal site

item.title: The long hauler phenomena: Why COVID19 mortality is only part of the story
item.description: Introduction There has been quite a lot of focus on using the infection fatality rate (IFR)1 of COVID19 to quantify the disease severity. The first thing to note here is that despite the math being simple, this is surprisingly hard to calculate2. One reason is that the total number of cases is unkown. However even if that were known, the actual number is not static3. With that said, the CDC’s current best guess of the overall infection fatality rate (IFR) is 0.

item.title: Modelling personal risk of COVID19 part 1: Why large groups are a really bad idea right now
item.description: Introduction I—like everyone else in the world—wants to be done this pandemic and to just get on with things. Unfortunately, COVID19 has other ideas. The reality is we’re just going to have to live it with for the time being. This of course varies from country to country. I’m an American who lives in Pennsylvania with family and friends in various locations scattered throughout the U.S. So everything I write will necessarily be colored by that perspective.
